summary: Feature completes the Settings page with company profile editing (name+brief), defaults display (company_type, brand_color, slug), onboarding replay/reset, backend status, and a danger zone with wallet disconnect confirmation. The useCompanySettings hook was extended with an update mutation and additional fields. Tests cover 12 settings scenarios.

strengths: Company profile editing uses a proper mutation with optimistic invalidation of both company-settings and active-company queries
Danger zone disconnect has a two-step confirmation pattern preventing accidental actions
Settings sections are well-organized into logical cards: Company Info, Backend Status, Tour, Onboarding, Defaults, Danger Zone
Extended CompanySettings interface with optional fields maintains backward compatibility

concerns.description: The disconnect wallet confirm handler mutates with { name: company?.name ?? '' } which updates the name to itself but doesn't actually clear wallet_address. The actual wallet disconnect would require a different mutation payload. This appears to be a placeholder that shows a success toast without truly disconnecting.

nonBlockingIssues.description: The wallet disconnect button in the danger zone performs a no-op mutation (updates name to itself) rather than actually clearing the wallet_address field. This is acceptable as wallet disconnection should happen through the auth provider, but the UX could be misleading.
